one_line_description: Google's advertising platform for search, display, YouTube and shopping campaigns, managed programmatically via the Google Ads API.
auth_summary: All API calls require OAuth2 (user consent flow or service account/domain-wide delegation with scope https://www.googleapis.com/auth/adwords) PLUS a Google Ads developer token attached to a manager (MCC) account. OAuth alone is not sufficient.
access_notes: Developer token is issued instantly but starts at Test Account Access (test accounts only). Google may auto-upgrade to Explorer Access (limited production ops/day); Basic Access (15k ops/day) and Standard Access (unlimited) require an application form review with business justification and API policy compliance.
api_surface_summary: Full campaign lifecycle: accounts, campaigns, ad groups, ads, budgets, bidding, audiences, keywords, conversions, and reporting via GAQL query language. Available as REST and gRPC with official client libraries.
main_blocker: Developer token access-level approval (Basic/Standard) required before production accounts can be used at scale
agent_notes: Google published an official open-source MCP server (googleads/google-ads-mcp, verified by fetching the repo) — initial version is read-only/diagnostics-focused and still requires your own developer token (Explorer+ for production) and OAuth credentials. Many community MCPs also exist. The developer token + MCC requirement is the practical hurdle for any multi-tenant toolkit.
